> The graph holds this event under its in-world name ("abduction") and models the dominant realm-narrative
> via [`rhaegar-targaryen SUSPECTED_OF abduction-of-lyanna`](../../edges/edges.jsonl) (Tier-2 — the
> in-world suspicion, NOT an assertion of force). The R+L=J reading (that Lyanna went willingly) is a GATED
> theory; the opposing on-page testimony is recorded here as substrate, not asserted.

In [42 AC](wiki:42_AC), Aegon and his sister-wife, Princess [Rhaena](wiki:Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I)) were in the middle of a [royal progress](wiki:Royal_progress), but found themselves trapped at [Crakehall](wiki:Crakehall) by members of the [Faith of the Seven](wiki:Faith_of_the_Seven), which had [risen in rebellion](wiki:Faith_Militant_uprising) against [House Targaryen](wiki:House_Targaryen). At the same time, Aegon's father, King Aenys, died on [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone). Dowager Queen [Visenya](wiki:Visenya_Targaryen) brought her son and Aenys's half-brother, Prince [Maegor](wiki:Maegor_I_Targaryen), back from his exile in [Essos](wiki:Essos), and he declared himself king ahead of Aegon.(wiki:Aegon_the_Uncrowned's_rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_maegor_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0)(wiki:Aegon_the_Uncrowned's_rebellion.cite_ref-Rfabthe_sons_of_the_dragon.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1) When the [Poor Fellows](wiki:Poor_Fellows) who had besieged Crakehall departed to march on [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing), Aegon and Rhaena fled to [Casterly Rock](wiki:Casterly_Rock), where Lord [Lyman Lannister](wiki:Lyman_Lannister) protected them by extending [guest right](wiki:Guest_right). There, Aegon's twin daughters [Aerea](wiki:Aerea_Targaryen) and [Rhaella](wiki:Rhaella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon)) were born the following year.(wiki:Aegon_the_Uncrowned's_rebellion.cite_ref-Rfabthe_sons_of_the_dragon.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)(wiki:Aegon_the_Uncrowned's_rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafappendix:_targaryen_lineage.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)(wiki:Aegon_the_Uncrowned's_rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_maegor_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1)

After Maegor's coronation, Aegon found himself abandoned by many who he once considered his loyal friends. Throughout the realm, it was whispered that Aegon was weak like his father was, pointing out Aegon had never taken a [dragon](wiki:Dragon) of his own, whereas Maegor had claimed [Balerion the Black Dread](wiki:Balerion). In [43 AC](wiki:43_AC), while Maegor was in [Oldtown](wiki:Oldtown), Rhaena and Aegon took note of his long absence from the capital, and after a long journey managed to successfully infiltrate [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing). With several companions aiding them, Aegon and Rhaena entered one of the city gates in a [mule](wiki:Mule) [wagon](wiki:Wagon), concealed in the back under sacks of corn. Once in the city, they were given further aid by several collaborators who had grown weary of Maegor's persecutions. However, Aegon and Rhaena did not seize the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne), as they lacked the strength to hold it; they had come for dragons. Rhaena was reunited with her [Dreamfyre](wiki:Dreamfyre), and Aegon finally claimed a dragon, his late father's mount [Quicksilver](wiki:Quicksilver). The pair left the city on dragonback, and returned to the [westerlands](wiki:Westerlands) to raise an army and exact Aegon's claim to the Iron Throne.(wiki:Aegon_the_Uncrowned's_rebellion.cite_ref-Rfabthe_sons_of_the_dragon.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)

## Aftermath

## Aftermath

On hearing of the [fall of King's Landing](wiki:Fall_of_King's_Landing) during their march away from the city, Aemond was consumed by fury, and turned it on [House Strong](wiki:House_Strong) of Harrenhal. Though they were allied with the greens, Aemond already loathed House Strong in general due to the rumors that Ser [Harwin Strong](wiki:Harwin_Strong) had been the true father of Rhaenyra's elder Velaryon sons, who Aemond had a grudge against due to losing an eye in a childhood fight with them. He also never fully trusted Lord [Larys Strong](wiki:Larys_Strong), the greens' [spymaster](wiki:Master_of_whisperers). Aemond became convinced that House Strong must have collaborated with the blacks. Indeed, Larys had not been apprehended when the blacks took King's Landing, but simply disappeared. Furthermore, his granduncle Ser [Simon Strong](wiki:Simon_Strong), the [castellan](wiki:Castellan) of Harrenhal, had [surrendered the castle to Daemon](wiki:Assault_on_Harrenhal) without a fight. Though Simon protested his innocence, insisting that he could not possibly have held a siege against Caraxes, Aemond commanded that Simon be given a sword in a [trial by combat](wiki:Trial_by_combat). Aemond had been well-trained by Criston Cole himself, while Simon was an old man; the resulting duel was utterly one-sided. Aemond cut Simon to pieces, then fed his corpse to Vhagar.(wiki:Aemond's_march_on_Harrenhal.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_triumphant.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-6)

Aemond then proceeded to massacre every male of House Strong, down to the last little grandson. The heap made of their severed heads ultimately stood three feet tall. No man or boy was spared, not even [bastards](wiki:Bastard) - except only one woman, [Alys Rivers](wiki:Alys_Rivers), whom Aemond took as a [bed-mate](wiki:Paramour), ignoring all other women. Men claimed that she was a [sorceress](wiki:Magic) who had bewitched him.(wiki:Aemond's_march_on_Harrenhal.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_triumphant.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-7)

## Edges

— Daenerys Targaryen's received memory, AGOT Daenerys I (`sources/chapters/agot/agot-daenerys-01.md:41`) — The Targaryen-exile smuggling beat the Narrative Arc above paraphrases

> I built a fleet at [Robert's](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon) command, took [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone) in his name. Did he take my hand and say, "Well done, brother, whatever should I do without you?" No, he blamed me for letting [Willem Darry](wiki:Willem_Darry) steal away [Viserys](wiki:Viserys_Targaryen) and [the babe](wiki:Daenerys_Targaryen), as if I could have stopped it.(wiki:Assault_on_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Racokprologue.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-4)
>
> —[Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon) to [Cressen](wiki:Cressen)

In the hour of the wolf after [Daenerys](daenerys-targaryen) flees the pit on
[Drogon](drogon), Ser [Barristan Selmy](barristan-selmy) and [Skahaz the Shavepate](skahaz-mo-kandaq)
move against King [Hizdahr zo Loraq](hizdahr-zo-loraq) — whom they suspect of the
[poisoned locusts](poisoned-locusts) and the [Harpy killings](sons-of-the-harpy-insurgency).
Barristan kills the pit fighter [Khrazz](khrazz) in single combat and takes Hizdahr prisoner,

> "Harrion Karstark was captive here when we took the castle, did you know? I gave him all the Karhold men still with me and sent him off with Glover. I do hope nothing ill befell him at Duskendale . . . else Alys Karstark would be all that remains of Lord Rickard's progeny."

> —[Roose Bolton](roose-bolton) to [Jaime Lannister](jaime-lannister), ASOS Jaime V (`sources/chapters/asos/asos-jaime-05.md:129`) — book-cite. Roose's own chilling account: he dispatched the *rival* Karstark men with Glover toward Duskendale, then voices a soft, false concern for them. The strongest in-text basis for `roose-bolton SUSPECTED_OF battle-at-duskendale` — the deliberate spending of Stark-loyal northmen ahead of his defection.

> "Duskendale, on the narrow sea? Why would they go to Duskendale?" He'd shook his head, bewildered. "A third of my foot, lost for Duskendale?"

Ser [Criston Cole](wiki:Criston_Cole) lost eight hundred soldiers to Meleys's dragonfire. King [Aegon II Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_II_Targaryen) was incapacitated by his burns for a year; Prince [Aemond Targaryen](wiki:Aemond_Targaryen) became [regent](wiki:Regent) while his brother recovered. Aegon's injured dragon, [Sunfyre](wiki:Sunfyre), was flightless and too heavy to be moved, so was left behind in the fields of Rook's Rest to eat carcasses and livestock.

The loss of [Rhaenys Targaryen](wiki:Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aemon)) and her dragon, [Meleys](wiki:Meleys), was a severe blow to the [blacks](wiki:Blacks). Rhaenys's husband, Lord [Corlys Velaryon](wiki:Corlys_Velaryon), was enraged, and blamed her death on Queen [Rhaenyra Targaryen](wiki:Rhaenyra_Targaryen), stating that if the queen or her sons had gone with their dragons, his wife might be alive.

Lord [Walys Mooton](wiki:Walys_Mooton) later led the [retaking of Rook's Rest](wiki:Retaking_of_Rook's_Rest) for the blacks, but was slain in a failed attempt to kill Sunfyre.(wiki:Battle_at_Rook's_Rest.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_the_red_dragon_and_the_gold.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-6)

Lord Bolton arrives in the [north](wiki:North) with a girl he claims to be [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark), the youngest daughter of the late Lord [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark) of [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell). In reality, however, the girl is [Jeyne Poole](wiki:Jeyne_Poole), the daughter of [Vayon Poole](wiki:Vayon_Poole), the former steward of Winterfell.(wiki:Battle_in_the_ice.cite_ref-Radwd20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-2) Roose marries the fake Arya to his [bastard son](wiki:Bastardy), the recently legitimized [Ramsay Bolton](wiki:Ramsay_Snow) at [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell).(wiki:Battle_in_the_ice.cite_ref-Radwd37.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-10) Their presence at Winterfell is meant to force Stannis to march on them,(wiki:Battle_in_the_ice.cite_ref-Radwd32.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2) and indeed, after following the advice of Jon Snow, thereby securing the support of the [northern mountain clans](wiki:Northern_mountain_clans), and [retaking Deepwood Motte](wiki:Fight_by_Deepwood_Motte) from the [ironborn](wiki:Ironborn)—resulting in the capture of [Asha Greyjoy](wiki:Asha_Greyjoy)(wiki:Battle_in_the_ice.cite_ref-Radwd26.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-1)—Stannis leads his army from [Deepwood Motte](wiki:Deepwood_Motte) through the [wolfswood](wiki:Wolfswood) to [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell). Although the southron knights and lords oppose the march, the northern lords insist that Roose must be removed from Winterfell and "the Ned's" daughter rescued from Ramsay. The [march on Winterfell](wiki:March_on_Winterfell) is a hundred leagues long, and is estimated to take fifteen days. However, snows begin on the fourth day, and the harsh weather slow the army down significantly. After thirty-three days, they arrive at a [crofters' village](wiki:Crofters'_village), located three days from Winterfell, but find themselves snowed in. After eleven days, Arnolf Karstark arrives with four hundred and fifty men.(wiki:Battle_in_the_ice.cite_ref-Radwd62.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-3)

At Winterfell, the forces Roose Bolton has gathered—consisting of Houses [Ryswell](wiki:House_Ryswell), [Dustin](wiki:House_Dustin), [Frey](wiki:House_Frey), [Cerwyn](wiki:House_Cerwyn), [Hornwood](wiki:House_Hornwood), [Manderly](wiki:House_Manderly), and part of [House Umber](wiki:House_Umber)—suffer from divided loyalties, infighting, and a series of mysterious murders, which cause tension in the castle between the Freys, Boltons, Manderlys, and other northern houses. At first, [Theon Greyjoy](wiki:Theon_Greyjoy) is suspected to be the murderer, but that idea is quickly dismissed by Roose, who declares Theon to be too broken and weak to have carried out the deeds.(wiki:Battle_in_the_ice.cite_ref-Radwd46.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-1)

At [Castle Black](wiki:Castle_Black), the [red priestess](wiki:Red_priest) [Melisandre](wiki:Melisandre) sees a grey girl on a dying horse in her flames. Believing the girl to be Arya Stark, Lord Commander Jon Snow's younger half-sister, she enlists the help of [Mance Rayder](wiki:Mance_Rayder), the former [King-beyond-the-Wall](wiki:King-beyond-the-Wall), to rescue the girl.(wiki:Battle_in_the_ice.cite_ref-Radwd31.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_23-0) The girl eventually arrives at Castle Black, but she turns out to be [Alys Karstark](wiki:Alys_Karstark), who informs Jon Snow that her uncle Arnolf is plotting to betray Stannis.(wiki:Battle_in_the_ice.cite_ref-Radwd44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_24-0) Jon sends ravens to [Deepwood Motte](wiki:Deepwood_Motte) in an attempt to warn Stannis.(wiki:Battle_in_the_ice.cite_ref-Radwd49.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_25-0) At Winterfell, the tensions among the army finally erupt into a brawl (in which Lord [Wyman Manderly](wiki:Wyman_Manderly) becomes injured) and Manderly and Frey men are killed after [Little Walder Frey](wiki:Walder_Frey_(son_of_Merrett)) is found dead. In response, Roose sends out the forces of House Frey and House Manderly, to engage Stannis into battle at the crofters' village.(wiki:Battle_in_the_ice.cite_ref-Radwd51.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1)

Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) smashes the river lords' forces at the [Golden Tooth](wiki:Battle_in_the_hills_below_the_Golden_Tooth) and [Riverrun](wiki:Battle_under_the_walls_of_Riverrun), capturing the heir to the riverlands, Ser [Edmure Tully](wiki:Edmure_Tully), and many of his [knights](wiki:Knight).(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Ragot56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0) Jaime besieges [Riverrun](wiki:Riverrun),(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Ragot56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-1) the capital of the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands), while the host of his father, Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister), closes the [kingsroad](wiki:Kingsroad) to the east(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Ragot55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0) and methodically captures the castles of river lords.(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Ragot56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-2)

Unbeknownst to Jaime, [Robb Stark](wiki:Robb_Stark) has forged an alliance with [Walder Frey](wiki:Walder_Frey), [Lord of the Crossing](wiki:Lord_of_the_Crossing), securing passage over the [Green Fork](wiki:Green_Fork) of the [Trident](wiki:Trident).(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Ragot59.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-0) Joined by additional soldiers from [House Frey](wiki:House_Frey) of the [Twins](wiki:Twins) and [House Mallister](wiki:House_Mallister) of [Seagard](wiki:Seagard), Robb moves on Riverrun from the north in an attempt to outflank the unsuspecting Lannister forces and relieve the besieged forces at Riverrun. Robb manages to keep his march secret, thanks to the efforts of his great uncle, Ser [Brynden Tully](wiki:Brynden_Tully), and three hundred picked outriders, who shoot down Lannister [ravens](wiki:Raven) and slay Jaime's scouts.(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Ragot63.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-22) Ser [Marq Piper](wiki:Marq_Piper) separately harries the Lannister supply trains with half a hundred men.(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Ragot69.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-0) Jaime leaves his camp at Riverrun three times to deal with raiders or stubborn holdfasts.(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Ragot63.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-23)

## Narrative Arc

Robb sends Brynden with a few hundred men carrying [Tully](wiki:House_Tully) banners to raid and draw Jaime from his camp at Riverrun. Expecting to crush a small force, Jaime and most of his cavalry are lured north of the [Tumblestone](wiki:Tumblestone) to a [forested valley](wiki:Whispering_Wood) where the rest of Robb's men are hidden.(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Ragot63.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-24) Lady [Maege Mormont](wiki:Maege_Mormont) and Lord [Jason Mallister](wiki:Jason_Mallister) wait east of the valley, while Lord [Rickard Karstark](wiki:Rickard_Karstark) is to the north. West is Ser [Stevron Frey](wiki:Stevron_Frey), and across a far ridge Lord [Greatjon Umber](wiki:Jon_Umber). Robb's mother, Lady [Catelyn Stark](wiki:Catelyn_Stark), waits high on a ridge under the protection of thirty guards led by [Hallis Mollen](wiki:Hallis_Mollen).(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Ragot63.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-25)

Once all of Jaime's unsuspecting men have entered the valley, Catelyn hears the sound of [snow shrikes](wiki:Snow_shrike), possibly alerts by the waiting northmen. The [Mormonts](wiki:House_Mormont) sound horns to signal the moonlit trap has been sprung, followed by [Umbers](wiki:House_Umber), [Mallisters](wiki:House_Mallister), Freys, and [Karstarks](wiki:House_Karstark). Jaime's horsemen are attacked by bowmen hidden in the trees by Robb, and the Young Wolf and northern bannermen lead their own cavalry against the ambushed [westermen](wiki:Westermen). Robb is joined in the fighting by his [direwolf](wiki:Direwolf), [Grey Wind](wiki:Grey_Wind).(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Ragot63.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-26) [Theon Greyjoy](wiki:Theon_Greyjoy), Robb's friend, kills men in the battle.(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Racok24.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-0)

When Jaime sees the battle is lost, he rallies his retainers and attempts to cut his way through the host to slay Robb in single combat. He is halted, but not before slaying [Daryn Hornwood](wiki:Daryn_Hornwood), [Eddard Karstark](wiki:Eddard_Karstark), and [Torrhen Karstark](wiki:Torrhen_Karstark) of [Robb's personal guard](wiki:Robb's_personal_guard).(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Ragot63.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-27) Grey Wind howls as dawn breaks and the fighting winds down.(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Ragot63.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-28)

According to Theon, the Lannisters lose ten men for every one of Robb's who fall.(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Ragot63.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-29) Jaime is captured, along with near a hundred knights and a dozen lords bannermen including Lord [Gawen Westerling](wiki:Gawen_Westerling), Lord [Quenten Banefort](wiki:Quenten_Banefort), Ser [Garth Greenfield](wiki:Garth_Greenfield), Lord [Regenard Estren](wiki:Regenard_Estren), Ser [Tytos Brax](wiki:Tytos_Brax), [Mallor the Dornishman](wiki:Mallor_the_Dornishman), [Willem Lannister](wiki:Willem_Lannister), and the Lannister cousins [Cleos Frey](wiki:Cleos_Frey) and [Tion Frey](wiki:Tion_Frey).(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Ragot63.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-30)

The capture of Jaime leaves the Lannister army surrounding Riverrun leaderless, leading directly to its disintegration at the [Battle of the Camps](wiki:Battle_of_the_Camps) and leaving Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) and Queen Regent [Cersei Lannister](wiki:Cersei_Lannister) despondent.(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Ragot69.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-1)(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Racok3.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_20-0) Rumors reach [Casterly Rock](wiki:Casterly_Rock) that [Grey Wind](wiki:Grey_Wind) tore out Jaime's throat in the battle.(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Raffc33.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_21-0)

Having Jaime as a hostage, and recognizing his value as a prisoner, the Starks gain enormous bargaining power. The deaths of Eddard and Torrhen Karstark by Jaime's hand plant a seed of vengeance in the heart of their father, Lord [Rickard Karstark](wiki:Rickard_Karstark), however.(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Ragot69.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-2)(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Ragot71.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_22-0) Gawen is taken to [Seagard](wiki:Seagard), Quenten to [Stone Hedge](wiki:Stone_Hedge), Garth to [Raventree Hall](wiki:Raventree_Hall), and Regenard and Tytos to the [Twins](wiki:Twins). Willem, Cleos, and Tion are held hostage at Riverrun.(wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood.cite_ref-Rasosappendix.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_23-0)

## Aftermath

The Lannister army's defeat is so great that, according to rumor, Robb cut out Stafford's heart and fed it to Grey Wind. Remnants fall back to Lannisport, where Stafford's son, Ser [Daven Lannister](wiki:Daven_Lannister), reforms them.(wiki:Battle_of_Oxcross.cite_ref-Racok39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-6)(wiki:Battle_of_Oxcross.cite_ref-Rasos19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0) Robb continues attacking the westerlands, capturing [Ashemark](wiki:Taking_of_Ashemark), and marches on to take the [Crag](wiki:Storming_of_the_Crag).(wiki:Battle_of_Oxcross.cite_ref-Racok35.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-3) [Galbart Glover](wiki:Galbart_Glover) and [Rickard Karstark](wiki:Rickard_Karstark) raid along [the coast](wiki:Raiding_along_the_westerlands_coast). Lady [Maege Mormont](wiki:Maege_Mormont) captures thousands of livestock and begins driving them back towards the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands). Lord [Greatjon Umber](wiki:Jon_Umber) captures [the gold mines](wiki:Seizure_of_westerlands_gold_mines) at [Nunn's Deep](wiki:Nunn's_Deep), [Castamere](wiki:Castamere), and in the [Pendric Hills](wiki:Pendric_Hills).(wiki:Battle_of_Oxcross.cite_ref-Racok39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-7)
[Lancel Lannister](wiki:Lancel_Lannister) proclaims in the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep) that Robb used "vile sorcery" to win, after which [northmen](wiki:Northmen) ate [enemy flesh](wiki:Cannibalism).(wiki:Battle_of_Oxcross.cite_ref-Racok32.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-9) Lord Tywin, having heard of the defeat at Oxcross, and feeling confident that [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon) laying [siege to Storm's End](wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End_(299)) means a secure southern front,(wiki:Battle_of_Oxcross.cite_ref-Racok44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0) marches west to face Robb's host.(wiki:Battle_of_Oxcross.cite_ref-Racok45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0)
The singer [Rymund the Rhymer](wiki:Rymund_the_Rhymer) composes a tune commemorating the northern victory, "[Wolf in the Night](wiki:Wolf_in_the_Night)".(wiki:Battle_of_Oxcross.cite_ref-Racok45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-1)

Using some vile sorcery, [your brother](wiki:Robb_Stark) fell upon Ser [Stafford Lannister](wiki:Stafford_Lannister) with an army of [wargs](wiki:Warg), not three days ride from [Lannisport](wiki:Lannisport). Thousands of good men were butchered as they slept, without the chance to lift sword. After the slaughter, the [northmen](wiki:Northmen) feasted on the [flesh of the slain](wiki:Cannibalism).(wiki:Battle_of_Oxcross.cite_ref-Racok32.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-10)
Do not take Oxcross too much to heart, my lady. A battle is not a war, and [my lord father](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) is assuredly not [my uncle Stafford](wiki:Stafford_Lannister).(wiki:Battle_of_Oxcross.cite_ref-Racok32.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-11)
Martyn: There's some say that after the battle, [the king](wiki:Robb_Stark) cut out [Stafford Lannister](wiki:Stafford_Lannister)'s heart and fed it to [the wolf](wiki:Grey_Wind). Catelyn: I would not believe such tales. My son is no savage.(wiki:Battle_of_Oxcross.cite_ref-Racok39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-8)

Even war-trained destriers went mad. Knights were trampled to death in their pavilions, and the rabble woke in terror and fled, casting aside their weapons to run the faster. Ser Stafford was slain as he chased after a horse. Lord Rickard Karstark drove a lance through his chest."* — `acok-sansa-03:153`. Ser [Brynden Tully](wiki:Brynden_Tully), leading Robb's van, attacks the Lannister camp north of the [Tumblestone](wiki:Tumblestone) at night. His force clears palisades and finishes off any remaining scouts, and the sleeping, leaderless camp is overrun. Men from the other camps, roused by the noise, soon attempt to come to the aid of the camp under attack. Lord [Andros Brax](wiki:Andros_Brax) leads his men from the middle camp to the rafts and attempts to make a crossing. Riverrun begins pelting the rafts with thrown rocks, however, and soon the rafts are flipping and sinking. Among those drowned is Lord Brax.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Camps.cite_ref-Ragot69.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-8)

Meanwhile, Robb leads two columns of armored horse, including [Umbers](wiki:House_Umber) and [Mallisters](wiki:House_Mallister), from the west in overrunning the camp between the rivers. [Grey Wind](wiki:Grey_Wind), Robb's [direwolf](wiki:Direwolf), allegedly kills four men and a dozen horses. The Lannisters in the middle camp form a shieldwall and repel one charge, but they are then taken in the rear by a sortie from Riverrun led by Tytos Blackwood. Lord [Jon Umber](wiki:Jon_Umber) burns the siege towers which were under construction, and Lord Blackwood releases the prisoners taken in the earlier [battle under the walls of Riverrun](wiki:Battle_under_the_walls_of_Riverrun), including Edmure.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Camps.cite_ref-Ragot69.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-9) Despite his condition, Lord Hoster watches the fighting from the battlements of Riverrun.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Camps.cite_ref-Ragot71.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-5)

In the southern camp of the Lannister force, the [Tyroshi sellsword](wiki:Tyroshi_sellsword) who leads the [freeriders](wiki:Freerider) of Ser [Forley Prester](wiki:Forley_Prester) strikes his banners and switches sides. Forley leads the retreat of two thousand spears and two thousand bowmen when he sees the battle is lost.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Camps.cite_ref-Ragot69.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-10)

[According](wiki:The_Testimony_of_Mushroom) to [Mushroom](wiki:Mushroom), [Hugh the Hammer](wiki:Hugh_Hammer) and [Ulf the White](wiki:Ulf_the_White) celebrated their participation. [Nettles](wiki:Nettles) and [Addam Velaryon](wiki:Addam_Velaryon) were heavily affected, with the latter seeking out the Sea Snake, though what they spoke to each other remains unknown.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Gullet.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_the_red_dragon_and_the_gold.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-15) Many people on Dragonstone came to resent Queen [Rhaenyra Targaryen](wiki:Rhaenyra_Targaryen) for kin they had lost during the battle.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Gullet.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_overthrown.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)

Only twenty-eight of the ninety ships commanded by [Sharako Lohar](wiki:Sharako_Lohar) returned home, and only three of those were not crewed by [Lyseni](wiki:Lys).(wiki:Battle_of_the_Gullet.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_the_red_dragon_and_the_gold.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-16) The Gullet was considered a disaster by the Triarchy.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Gullet.cite_ref-Rfabunder_the_regents_-_the_hooded_hand.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0) The Myrish and Tyroshi widows accused Sharako of sending their fleets to destruction while holding back his own Lysene ships, thus beginning a quarrel that would eventually lead to the [Daughters' War](wiki:Daughters'_War).(wiki:Battle_of_the_Gullet.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_the_red_dragon_and_the_gold.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-17) A disgraced Sharako discretely sold the captive Viserys to Magister [Bambarro Bazanne](wiki:Bambarro_Bazanne) of Lys.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Gullet.cite_ref-Rfabunder_the_regents_-_the_voyage_of_alyn_oakenfist.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0)

Ultimately the battle was very costly for both sides, and could be considered both a tactical victory for the greens and a strategic win for the blacks. Whereas the death of Prince Jacaerys deprived the blacks of one of their own commanders and Queen Rhaenyra of her eldest heir, the greens had failed in their main goal: to break the hold of the Velaryon fleet on the east coasts of Westeros. Moreover, the greens' allies in the southern Free Cities took such heavy losses that they played no further part in the war.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Gullet.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_overthrown.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-1)

## Edges

For their part, the [Faith Militant](wiki:Faith_Militant) remained active. Thousands of [Poor Fellows](wiki:Poor_Fellows) haunted roads and remote locations in the [Reach](wiki:Reach) and the [Vale](wiki:Vale), as well as the lands surrounding the [Trident](wiki:Trident), in small bands that fell upon travelers and swarmed towns, villages and poorly defended castles, killing whatever king's loyalists they came upon. With Ser [Horys Hill](wiki:Horys_Hill) lost much of his support after Faith Militant's defeat at the Great Fork, new leaders arose among the Poor Fellows, such as [Ragged Silas](wiki:Ragged_Silas), [Septon Moon](wiki:Septon_Moon) and [Dennis the Lame](wiki:Dennis_the_Lame). Meanwhile, the [Warrior's Sons](wiki:Warrior's_Sons) elected Ser [Joffrey Doggett](wiki:Joffrey_Doggett) as their new [grand captain](wiki:Grand_captain). Ser Joffrey and a hundred men set out from their chapterhouse in [Lannisport](wiki:Lannisport) to seek the blessing of the [High Septon](wiki:High_Septon). By the time the Warrior's Sons arrived at [Oldtown](wiki:Oldtown), their numbers rose to two thousand, among [knights](wiki:Knight), [squires](wiki:Squire) and [freeriders](wiki:Freerider). Elsewhere, lords and followers of the Faith gathered men and plotted ways to bring down the crown.(wiki:Burning_of_the_seats_of_the_pious_lords.cite_ref-Rfabthe_sons_of_the_dragon.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1)

The crown was aware of these events, and [ravens](wiki:Raven) were sent to summon lords and [landed knights](wiki:Landed_knight) suspected of disloyalty to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) to bend the knee, do homage to the king and present a son or daughter as [hostage](wiki:Hostage). The Faith Militant, for their part, were [outlawed](wiki:Outlaw), with [membership declared punishable by death](wiki:King_Maegor's_laws), and the High Septon was commanded to deliver himself to the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep) to stand trial under charges of high treason. The High Septon's response was a summon to the king, commanding him to present himself to the [Starry Sept](wiki:Starry_Sept) to beg the forgiveness of the [gods](wiki:Seven-Faced_God) for his sins and cruelties. ## Origins

[Duskendale](wiki:Duskendale), which had once been an important port on [Blackwater Bay](wiki:Blackwater_Bay) and a seat of kings, had seen its wealth shrink and trade dwindle due to the growth of nearby [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing). [Denys Darklyn](wiki:Denys_Darklyn), Lord of Duskendale, wished to halt this decline. He desired to win a charter for Duskendale, similar as [Dorne](wiki:Dorne) had received nearly a century before. Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister), [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), however, firmly rejected the proposal. Infuriated at Tywin's refusal, Denys devised a new plan, which would eventually have severe consequences. Some say Lord Denys had been driven by the influence of his [Myrish](wiki:Myr) wife, Lady [Serala](wiki:Serala).(wiki:Defiance_of_Duskendale.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aerys_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-0)

## Aftermath

King [Aerys II Targaryen](wiki:Aerys_II_Targaryen)'s vengeance was terrible. Though Lord [Denys Darklyn](wiki:Denys_Darklyn) begged for mercy, the king demanded the deaths of Denys and his immediate family, as well as his uncles, aunts, and distant [House Darklyn](wiki:House_Darklyn) kinsmen living in Duskendale. [House Hollard](wiki:House_Hollard), Lord Denys's goodkin, were attainted and executed as well. Ser [Jon Hollard](wiki:Jon_Hollard), who had been married to Denys's sister, died together with his wife and young son. [Robin Hollard](wiki:Robin_Hollard), the squire who had pulled Aerys's beard when he had first been seized, died upon the rack. The only exception was young [Dontos Hollard](wiki:Dontos_Hollard), whose life was spared when Ser [Barristan Selmy](wiki:Barristan_Selmy) requested it. Aerys, unable to refuse the man who had saved his life, granted this boon. Denys's wife, Lady [Serala](wiki:Serala), had her tongue and womanly parts torn out, after which she was burned alive.(wiki:Defiance_of_Duskendale.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aerys_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-9)(wiki:Defiance_of_Duskendale.cite_ref-Raffc9.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-4) The lands and incomes of [Duskendale](wiki:Duskendale) were granted to [House Rykker](wiki:House_Rykker), while the lands of [House Hollard](wiki:House_Hollard) were taken away, their villages burned, and their castle torn down.(wiki:Defiance_of_Duskendale.cite_ref-Raffc9.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-5)
Despite what happened, the people of Duskendale still love Lord Denys. They blame his foreign wife, Serala, and half of them still feel that the punishment Aerys gave her was too kind by half.(wiki:Defiance_of_Duskendale.cite_ref-Raffc9.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-6)
Following his half year of captivity at Duskendale, Aerys II was forever a changed man. It is believed by some that it was the Defiance which caused the king's madness.(wiki:Defiance_of_Duskendale.cite_ref-Raffc9.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-7) Barristan, however, claims that it had always been in him, with the lapses of madness growing more frequent over time,(wiki:Defiance_of_Duskendale.cite_ref-Rasos71.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) and it is considered that the Defiance shattered what little sanity Aerys had left.(wiki:Defiance_of_Duskendale.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aerys_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-10) His madness grew significantly worse in the years that followed. He would no longer allow others to touch him, and as a result, his hair and fingernails grew longer and longer. No blades were allowed near him, except for the swords of his [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard), and his judgments became more severe and crueler. From the end of the Defiance in [277 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), until the [tourney at Harrenhal](wiki:Tourney_at_Harrenhal) in [281 AC](wiki:281_AC), Aerys refused to leave the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep).(wiki:Defiance_of_Duskendale.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aerys_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-11)(wiki:Defiance_of_Duskendale.cite_ref-Radwd67.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)
Furthermore, his experience at Duskendale had convinced Aerys that his son and heir, Prince [Rhaegar Targaryen](wiki:Rhaegar_Targaryen), had conspired with Tywin to have Aerys killed during the Defiance by storming the walls, which would have allowed for Rhaegar to ascend the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) and marry Lord Tywin's daughter, [Cersei Lannister](wiki:Cersei_Lannister). Aerys grew mistrustful of Tywin as a consequence, going as far as to refuse to meet with Tywin without the presence of all his Kingsguard knights. Relations with his son Rhaegar became more strained, and the king began to mistrust everyone, from the [smallfolk](wiki:Smallfolk) to his own wife, Queen [Rhaella Targaryen](wiki:Rhaella_Targaryen).(wiki:Defiance_of_Duskendale.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aerys_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-12)

Lord [Gerold Lannister](wiki:Gerold_Lannister) had four sons with his second wife, Lady [Rohanne Webber](wiki:Rohanne_Webber); the eldests were a set of twins, [Tywald](wiki:Tywald_Lannister) and [Tion](wiki:Tion_Lannister) (b. 210's AC), followed by [Tytos](wiki:Tytos_Lannister) (b. [220 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest)) and then [Jason](wiki:Jason_Lannister_(son_of_Gerold)) (b. [229 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest)).(wiki:Double_wedding_at_Casterly_Rock.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_westerlands:_house_lannister_under_the_dragons.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1)
Several years prior to the [Peake Uprising](wiki:Peake_Uprising) of [233 AC](wiki:233_AC), Tywald was betrothed to [Ellyn Reyne](wiki:Ellyn_Reyne), the daughter of [Robert Reyne](wiki:Robert_Reyne), [Lord of Castamere](wiki:Lord_of_Castamere). When Lord [Peake](wiki:House_Peake) of [Starpike](wiki:Starpike) rose in rebellion against King [Maekar I Targaryen](wiki:Maekar_I_Targaryen) a host of [westermen](wiki:Westermen) aided Maekar to put down the rebellion. Tywald served as Lord Robert's squire during the conflict but both died during the [storming of Starpike](wiki:Storming_of_Starpike), as well as King Maekar. Prince [Aegon Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_V_Targaryen) [knighted](wiki:Knight) Tywald as he laid dying in Tion's arms. Later that year, Lord Gerold made the journey to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) to the assembled [Great Council](wiki:Great_Council) to elect Maekar's successor to the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne). Gerold's eloquence (and some say [gold](wiki:Gold)) helped Prince Aegon to win the election. Born the fourth son of a fourth son, Aegon became known as "Aegon the Unlikely" from that day forth, and he ruled as Aegon V Targaryen.(wiki:Double_wedding_at_Casterly_Rock.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_westerlands:_house_lannister_under_the_dragons.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)(wiki:Double_wedding_at_Casterly_Rock.cite_ref-sample_2-1)
Grieving the death of his heir and the disappearance of his wife three years earlier, Lord Gerold became ill and a pale shadow of the man once dubbed "Gerold the Golden". Not abandoning her ambition to become Lady of Casterly Rock someday, Lady Ellyn took advantage of Gerold's apparent weakness to convince Tion to marry her in place of his late twin. Lord Gerold opposed the match at first but then agreed.(wiki:Double_wedding_at_Casterly_Rock.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_westerlands:_house_lannister_under_the_dragons.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)(wiki:Double_wedding_at_Casterly_Rock.cite_ref-sample_2-2)

## Identity

After [Aeron Damphair](aeron-greyjoy) [vanishes vowing to raise the ironborn smallfolk](aeron-vows-to-raise-the-ironborn-smallfolk) against the godless king, [Euron Greyjoy](euron-greyjoy) sets [Erik Ironmaker](erik-ironmaker) to search the isles for the missing priest — Euron's response to the one rival who refused to submit and whose prophet's voice can turn the common folk against him. [Tristifer Botley](tristifer-botley) believes the search is a sham: that Euron has already had Aeron's throat cut and stages the hunt only to mask the kinslaying, since "Euron is afraid to be seen as a kinslayer." **The published books leave Aeron's fate unresolved** — he is missing and hunted, neither confirmed dead nor found (so no `death-of-aeron` node is minted; the murder is an in-world rumor, not canon). Downstream — Aeron's eventual capture by Euron — is a TWOW beat not in the corpus, left dark.

## Aftermath

Prince Jaehaerys ascended the throne in [48 AC](wiki:48_AC) following Maegor's death. He was crowned by the High Septon, and he declared an amnesty for the surviving Faith Militant. While he wished to disband the holy orders, Jaehaerys appointed [Joffrey Doggett](wiki:Joffrey_Doggett) to his [kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard) where he served faithfully for many years. Septon [Moon](wiki:Moon) and his less reputable rabble of followers marched on [Oldtown](wiki:Oldtown) to try to have him formally declared High Septon, but he received no support from major lords. Moon was assassinated in the night and his followers quickly dispersed (many suspect Moon's death was ordered by the Hightowers but accounts differ).

The first dozen years of young Jaehaerys's reign would be spent trying to reunite a realm strained by his uncle's tyranny, though thankfully Jaehaerys proved to be an adept diplomat. Moreover the royal treasury was seriously depleted, so Jaehaerys had to take out massive loans from the [Iron Bank of Braavos](wiki:Iron_Bank_of_Braavos) and then spend years adjusting the tax code to try to pay off the interest without inflaming the lords of the realm to rebellion again.

### Baelor I and Viserys II

After King [Baelor I Targaryen](wiki:Baelor_I_Targaryen) made peace with [Dorne](wiki:Dorne), he returned to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) and annulled his marriage to his eldest sister, [Daena](wiki:Daena_Targaryen). He then locked Daena with his other sisters, [Rhaena](wiki:Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon_III)) and [Elaena](wiki:Elaena_Targaryen), in the [Maidenvault](wiki:Maidenvault), so they could not tempt the lusts of men. However, Daena contrived to escape her imprisonment on several occasions, and towards the end of her brother's reign became pregnant by her cousin, Prince [Aegon](wiki:Aegon_IV_Targaryen).(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_baelor_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) In late [170 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), Daena gave birth to a [bastard](wiki:Bastard) son whom she named [Daemon Waters](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre).(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_baelor_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-1)(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0) Daena refused to reveal the identity of the father of her child, though Aegon was already suspected to have been involved.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-1) King Baelor, in response to Daemon's birth, began a forty-day fast, which eventually caused his death in [171 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest).(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_baelor_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-2) After the claims of Daena and her sisters were dismissed, Daena's uncle was crowned as King [Viserys II Targaryen](wiki:Viserys_II_Targaryen). Viserys ruled only for a year, until his own death, after which his eldest son, King [Aegon IV Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_IV_Targaryen), ascended the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) in [172 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest).(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_viserys_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0)

### Aegon IV

In addition to his unacknowledged bastard son by [Daena](wiki:Daena_Targaryen), King [Aegon IV Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_IV_Targaryen) had by his marriage to his sister, [Naerys](wiki:Naerys_Targaryen), a trueborn son named [Daeron](wiki:Daeron_II_Targaryen), born in [153 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), and a daughter, [Daenerys](wiki:Daenerys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon_IV)), born in [172 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest). Aegon often clashed with Prince Daeron during his reign, with Daeron opposing the ideas and the corrupt style of rule of his father. Aegon, on the other hand, despised his sister-wife, Naerys, and his younger brother, Prince [Aemon the Dragonknight](wiki:Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II)), to both of whom Prince Daeron was close. When Aegon, in [174 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), planned to launch an unprovoked attack against [Dorne](wiki:Dorne), the homeland of his daughter-in-law [Myriah Martell](wiki:Myriah_Martell), Daeron opposed his ideas. Around the same time, rumors began to circulate, instigated by King Aegon himself, that Daeron was a bastard born of adultery between his siblings, Naerys and Aemon. For the first time, though not the last, Aegon threatened to name one of his bastard sons ([Aegor Rivers](wiki:Aegor_Rivers) and [Balerion Otherys](wiki:Balerion_Otherys) being the only sons Aegon had, at this point, acknowledged) as his heir over Daeron.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-2) The rumors about Daeron's parentage forever remained, but despite Aegon's threats and japes, Daeron was never formally disowned. Meanwhile, two factions began to form at court, one supporting the corrupt reign of Aegon IV, the other supporting Daeron.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-3)

In [182 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), Aegon knighted his bastard son, [Daemon Waters](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre), after the boy won a squire's tourney at the age of twelve. Aegon publicly bestowed [Blackfyre](wiki:Blackfyre), one of the [Valyrian steel](wiki:Valyrian_steel) swords of [House Targaryen](wiki:House_Targaryen), on Daemon as well,(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-4) finally acknowledging him.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-greatbastards_13-0) There were some who felt that the sword symbolized the monarchy(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-14) (not unlike rumors that were spread when King [Aenys I Targaryen](wiki:Aenys_I_Targaryen) bestowed the sword on his half-brother, Prince [Maegor](wiki:Maegor_I_Targaryen)),(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-15) as it had been the sword of King [Aegon I Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_I_Targaryen), the Conqueror, and had been passed on from king to king ever since. Aegon's public gift of the sword eventually started talk that Daemon should be king.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-targaryenhistory_16-0) Following the acquisition of the sword, Daemon took the name "[Blackfyre](wiki:House_Blackfyre)" for himself.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-5)

### Daeron II

Dissatisfaction grew from the fact that Dorne had won several concessions during Maron's negotiations that the other regions did not have (e.g. the right to keep their [royal title](wiki:Prince_of_Dorne), autonomy to maintain their own laws, the right to assess and gather the taxes due to the Iron Throne with only irregular oversight from the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep)). Additionally, it was the belief that the Dornish held too much power at court, as many Dornishmen were brought to Daeron's court, and several filled important offices.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-5)

While Daeron had chosen to be crowned with his father's crown, rumors about his legitimacy remained. Daeron was not a warrior, and while Prince [Baelor](wiki:Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II)), Daeron's eldest son by his Dornish wife [Myriah Martell](wiki:Myriah_Martell), was all that could be desired in a warrior, Baelor had the looks of a [Martell](wiki:House_Martell), and not, like Daemon Blackfyre, of a Targaryen. Nobles and knights from the [Dornish Marches](wiki:Dornish_Marches) began to mistrust both Daeron and Baelor, and began to long for the days when the Dornish were still their enemies.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-6)

Meanwhile, Daemon Blackfyre had grown into a great warrior of his own, trained by Ser [Quentyn Ball](wiki:Quentyn_Ball),(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1) and had come to resemble [Aegon the Conqueror](wiki:Aegon_I_Targaryen) in appearance according to his supporters, with silver-gold hair and deep [purple eyes](wiki:Purple_eyes).(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-greatbastards_13-1) Married to Rohanne of Tyrosh in [184 AC](wiki:184_AC), his twin sons [Aegon](wiki:Aegon_Blackfyre) and [Aemon](wiki:Aemon_Blackfyre) were born later that same year.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-17) Five other sons, and at least two daughters followed during the years to come.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-2)(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtss.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-2) Daemon's supporters claimed that Daemon had desired his half-sister, Princess Daenerys, and the singers claim that she loved him as well. It is said that Aegon, who had seen more profit in the match with Tyrosh, had promised Daemon that he could have more than one bride. Daeron, however did not agree. Nor has it ever been proven that Princess Daenerys indeed loved Daemon, as the singers and Daemon's supporters claimed.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-7)

The Dornish concessions and influence, the sudden change of Dornish enemies to Dornish allies, Daeron's rumored illegitimacy, and Aegon IV's public gift of the sword of kings to Daemon, all were part of the seeds from which the first Blackfyre Rebellion grew. Still, it took many years for the actual rebellion to begin.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-8) There was no final insult that resulted in the declaration of Daemon's claim.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-9) Daemon had come to resent having the status of bastard, and what it represented.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-santafe_18-0) Additionally, he had councillors who urged him to rebel, and many famous warriors who disagreed with the peace with Dorne sought him out. Years passed between the first man approaching him, and Daemon finally declaring his claim. However, Maester [Yandel](wiki:Yandel) considers it likely that it had been [Aegor Rivers](wiki:Aegor_Rivers), Daemon's younger half-brother, who managed to convince Daemon in the end.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-10) According to [Maekar Targaryen](wiki:Maekar_I_Targaryen), it had been both Bittersteel and Fireball, the master-at-arms at the Red Keep, who had played the largest role in convincing Daemon.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-3) Aegor pressed him to proclaim, increasing his efforts after Daemon agreed to wed his eldest daughter, [Calla](wiki:Calla_Blackfyre), to Bittersteel himself, and many lords and knights joined in.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-11)

## Aftermath

### 281 AC[ ]

The announcement of the [tourney at Harrenhal](wiki:Tourney_at_Harrenhal) by Lord [Walter Whent](wiki:Walter_Whent) gave reason for suspicion. It was rumored that there had been a "shadow host", who had been providing Walter with the funds to pay the magnificent prizes which were promised. These rumors were widely believed in [281 AC](wiki:281_AC), and are still believed at the present. Whilst a dozen names have been put forward for the identity of this shadow host, Prince [Rhaegar Targaryen](wiki:Rhaegar_Targaryen) is the most compelling.(wiki:Great_Council.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_fall_of_the_dragons:_the_year_of_the_false_spring.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0)
Rhaegar allegedly arranged the tourney by sending Ser [Oswell Whent](wiki:Oswell_Whent), his friend, and Lord Walter's brother, as a go-between. Providing the gold for prizes, the prince wanted to lure as many lords and knights as possible to [Harrenhal](wiki:Harrenhal) to call an informal Great Council. Hoping to address the madness of his father, King [Aerys II Targaryen](wiki:Aerys_II_Targaryen), Rhaegar possibly desired a regency or a forced abdication.(wiki:Great_Council.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_fall_of_the_dragons:_the_year_of_the_false_spring.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-1)
Aerys, warned of these rumors by his [master of whisperers](wiki:Master_of_whisperers), Lord [Varys](wiki:Varys), decided to attend the tourney personally, despite not having left the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep) for four years.(wiki:Great_Council.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_fall_of_the_dragons:_the_year_of_the_false_spring.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-2)

## Identity

In King's Landing, Petyr "Littlefinger" Baelish tells Catelyn Stark that the assassin's Valyrian-steel dagger was won from him by Tyrion Lannister in a wager on Prince Joffrey's name-day tourney. This is a lie (the dagger's true provenance is later shown to be Littlefinger's own fabrication), and it is the **actual causal hinge** of Tyrion's arrest: without the false attribution, Catelyn has a suspicious blade but no named suspect. The catspaw's failed attack supplied the dagger; Littlefinger's lie aimed it at Tyrion.

## Identity

The inciting mystery of the entire saga. [Jon Arryn](jon-arryn), Hand of the King, was poisoned with the tears of Lys — a rare, clear, traceless poison. A letter from [Lysa Arryn](lysa-arryn) to her sister Catelyn blamed the Lannisters, pulling [Eddard Stark](eddard-stark) to King's Landing and triggering the whole chain of events in AGOT. The ASOS reveal (Lysa's confession to Littlefinger at the Eyrie) exposes [Lysa Arryn](lysa-arryn) as the direct administrator of the poison, acting at the instigation of [Petyr Baelish](petyr-baelish). [Cersei Lannister](cersei-lannister) was the prominent false-misdirection target named in Lysa's letter. This event is NOT part of Robert's Rebellion (it occurs 298 AC, roughly 15 years after RR's close); it is a standalone hub anchoring the War of the Five Kings' proximate trigger-chain.

## Identity

To destroy Queen [Margaery Tyrell](margaery-tyrell), [Cersei Lannister](cersei-lannister) sends [Ser Osney Kettleblack](osney-kettleblack) — her own lover — to the [High Septon](high-sparrow) at the [Great Sept of Baelor](great-sept-of-baelor) to confess that he has bedded the queen and her cousins, a scripted false accusation meant to deliver Margaery into the Faith's hands. The scheme backfires catastrophically. The High Sparrow does not simply accept the confession; he holds Osney and puts him to the Faith's scourge, and under the lash Osney recants the part about Margaery and tells a far more dangerous truth — that the queen who bedded him and sent him to smother the *previous* High Septon in his sleep was Cersei herself. The confession Cersei engineered to ruin Margaery becomes the evidence the armed Faith uses to seize and imprison Cersei. It is the pivot of her self-caused downfall.
