## Aftermath

A drunken Ser [Dontos Hollard](wiki:Dontos_Hollard) helps [Sansa Stark](wiki:Sansa_Stark) escape the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep), and [Oswell Kettleblack](wiki:Oswell_Kettleblack) rows them into [Blackwater Bay](wiki:Blackwater_Bay). When they reach a waiting ship, however, Lord [Petyr Baelish](wiki:Petyr_Baelish) has Dontos killed by Ser [Lothor Brune](wiki:Lothor_Brune)'s men.(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos61.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-1) Petyr and Sansa sail on *[Merling King](wiki:Merling_King)* to the [Fingers](wiki:Fingers). Once at [Petyr's tower](wiki:Drearfort), Sansa learns that her silver hair net secretly contained poison within its amethysts. [Olenna Tyrell](wiki:Olenna_Tyrell) took the poison from an unsuspecting Sansa when she straightened the girl's hair during the celebration. Sansa determines that the poison was then put into Joffrey's wine, and Petyr explains he had plotted Joffrey's death so that [Margaery Tyrell](wiki:Margaery_Tyrell) could marry Joffrey's younger brother, [Tommen Baratheon](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon).(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0) According to [George R. R. Martin](wiki:George_R._R._Martin), the conspirators wanted Joffrey to appear to die accidentally by choking, rather than a public assassination like the [Red Wedding](wiki:Red_Wedding).(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-EW_1-1)

[Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister), Sansa's husband, is also falsely implicated. While in custody, he believes that Sansa had poisoned Joffrey with other conspirators.(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0) At the start of his trial, Tyrion states that Joffrey had accidentally choked to death on the pie.(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-1) Maesters [Ballabar](wiki:Ballabar) and [Frenken](wiki:Frenken) find no food in Joffrey's throat and concur that he was poisoned, however, and Grand Maester [Pycelle](wiki:Pycelle) testifies that Tyrion killed the king with the [strangler](wiki:Strangler).(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-2) Lady [Taena Merryweather](wiki:Taena_Merryweather) swears she saw Tyrion drop something into Joffrey's [wedding chalice](wiki:Wedding_chalice), and [Lord Estermont](wiki:Lord_Estermont), [Josmyn Peckledon](wiki:Josmyn_Peckledon), [Galyeon of Cuy](wiki:Galyeon_of_Cuy), and [Morros](wiki:Morros_Slynt) and [Jothos Slynt](wiki:Jothos_Slynt) all recall having seen Tyrion empty the chalice of wine while the king died.(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-3)

### Randyll Tarly

The reports by survivors are passed on from the [Quiet Isle](wiki:Quiet_Isle) to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) and soon become widely distributed. The references to a brutal man wearing the [Hound](wiki:Hound)'s distinctive helm led people to the misperception that [Sandor Clegane](wiki:Sandor_Clegane) led the raid.(wiki:Raid_on_Saltpans.cite_ref-Raffc12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0)(wiki:Raid_on_Saltpans.cite_ref-Raffc16.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-1) Lord [Randyll Tarly](wiki:Randyll_Tarly), trying to restore order in the eastern [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands) from nearby [Maidenpool](wiki:Maidenpool), spreads rumors that blame the raid on [Beric Dondarrion](wiki:Beric_Dondarrion)'s [brotherhood without banners](wiki:Brotherhood_without_banners) in an attempt to turn the smallfolk against them. The term "Mad Dog of Saltpans" circulates,(wiki:Raid_on_Saltpans.cite_ref-Raffc25.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1) and as the tales spread, the number of murders that Sandor is accused of rises from twelve to twenty.(wiki:Raid_on_Saltpans.cite_ref-Raffc12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-1)(wiki:Raid_on_Saltpans.cite_ref-Raffc30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-2)

### House Frey

Queen Regent [Cersei Lannister](wiki:Cersei_Lannister) suggests that [Kevan Lannister](wiki:Kevan_Lannister)'s son, Lord [Lancel Lannister](wiki:Lancel_Lannister), go after [Beric Dondarrion](wiki:Beric_Dondarrion) and [Sandor Clegane](wiki:Sandor_Clegane) to restore order around the [Darry](wiki:Darry) lands, but Kevan tells her his son is not the man to deal with someone like the [Hound](wiki:Hound). Cersei proposes that Kevan hunt down Sandor, but he says the Hound was Cersei's creature and that it is her responsibility.(wiki:Raid_on_Saltpans.cite_ref-Raffc12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-2) Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) refuses to hunt down the Hound and suggests Lord [Randyll Tarly](wiki:Randyll_Tarly) deal with reestablishing order in the region. As Kevan heads towards Darry for the wedding of Lancel and [Amerei Frey](wiki:Amerei_Frey), Jaime cautions Kevan to keep his knights close, thinking of how dangerous the Hound was. However, Kevan interprets this as an implied threat by his nephew.(wiki:Raid_on_Saltpans.cite_ref-Raffc16.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-2)

When Cersei meets the [High Sparrow](wiki:High_Sparrow) at the [Great Sept of Baelor](wiki:Great_Sept_of_Baelor), this new [High Septon](wiki:High_Septon) refers to the death of Septon [Bennet](wiki:Bennet) and the rape of the twelve-year-old girl promised to the [Faith](wiki:Faith_of_the_Seven) as examples of the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) not being able to protect the faithful any longer. Cersei blames Sandor joining the band of Beric and insists King [Tommen I Baratheon](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon) cannot be blamed for all crimes committed by people once employed by [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister). Cersei agrees to the restoration of the [Faith Militant](wiki:Faith_Militant) in exchange for the High Septon acknowledging Tommen as king and forgiving the crown's debt to the Faith.(wiki:Raid_on_Saltpans.cite_ref-Raffc28.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-1)

At Darry, Ser [Arwood Frey](wiki:Arwood_Frey) tells Jaime of his futile attempt to find traces of the brotherhood without banners at Saltpans. The atrocities make Jaime skeptical that this was the Hound's doing, as, despite all Sandor's brutality, he did not consider him quite the monster his brother [Gregor](wiki:Gregor_Clegane) had been. Arwood assures Jaime that the identification of Sandor as leader of the raid is unmistakable according to all accounts. Jaime refuses the appeal by Amerei to hunt down the Hound for her, citing his obligations at [Riverrun](wiki:Riverrun). However, Ser [Lyle Crakehall](wiki:Lyle_Crakehall) promises her to return and kill the Hound for her once the [siege of Riverrun](wiki:Siege_of_Riverrun) is complete, making Jaime muse that the Strongboar is not strong enough to defeat Sandor.(wiki:Raid_on_Saltpans.cite_ref-Raffc27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)(wiki:Raid_on_Saltpans.cite_ref-Raffc30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-4)

### Brienne and the Crossroads Inn

In her search for [Sansa Stark](wiki:Sansa_Stark), information that she misinterprets leads [Brienne of Tarth](wiki:Brienne_of_Tarth) to the [Whispers](wiki:Whispers), where she comes across three former Brave Companions who had not participated in the raid, [Shagwell](wiki:Shagwell), [Timeon](wiki:Timeon) and [Pyg](wiki:Pyg). Before killing the three, she learns from Timeon that "the Stark girl" was actually in the [Hound](wiki:Hound)'s hands and that the [brotherhood](wiki:Brotherhood_without_banners) was searching for the two along the [Trident](wiki:Trident).(wiki:Raid_on_Saltpans.cite_ref-Raffc20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1) Believing this to refer to Sansa rather than to [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark), Brienne decides to look for the Hound herself. Upon her return to [Maidenpool](wiki:Maidenpool), she learns from Ser [Hyle Hunt](wiki:Hyle_Hunt) about [Sandor Clegane](wiki:Sandor_Clegane)'s alleged responsibility for the raid and the attempts to blame it on the brotherhood.(wiki:Raid_on_Saltpans.cite_ref-Raffc25.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)

Septon [Meribald](wiki:Meribald) leads them to the [Quiet Isle](wiki:Quiet_Isle), where the [Elder Brother](wiki:Elder_Brother_(Quiet_Isle)) shares his detailed knowledge of the raid of Saltpans, including the cowardice exhibited by Ser [Quincy Cox](wiki:Quincy_Cox). He informs Brienne that the Hound could not have been responsible, as he had buried the man himself, and that the "Stark girl" who had accompanied Sandor Clegane earlier had actually not been Sansa but Arya. The Elder Brother knows nothing of the girl's whereabouts, but suspects she might have been among those killed at Saltpans.(wiki:Raid_on_Saltpans.cite_ref-Raffc31.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-6) Brienne's small band finds nothing but death and destruction in Saltpans and they are refused entry to the holdfast. They head west towards the [crossroads inn](wiki:Crossroads_inn).(wiki:Raid_on_Saltpans.cite_ref-Raffc37.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0)

Meanwhile, the influence of the vengeful [Lady Stoneheart](wiki:Catelyn_Tully) has changed the brotherhood into a band that kills in large numbers. They use the inn to capture assumed offenders and hang them along nearby roads, often shoving pieces of salt into their mouths as reference to the raid on Saltpans. However, it is unclear whether the hanged were guilty of crimes or had just fought for the wrong side in the [War of the Five Kings](wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings).(wiki:Raid_on_Saltpans.cite_ref-Raffc37.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-1)(wiki:Raid_on_Saltpans.cite_ref-Raffc42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0) Decorations worn by condemned [broken men](wiki:Broken_men) include axes ([Byrch](wiki:House_Byrch) or [Cerwyn](wiki:House_Cerwyn)), arrows ([Norridge](wiki:House_Norridge) or [Sarsfield](wiki:House_Sarsfield)), salmon ([Mooton](wiki:House_Mooton)), pine trees ([Mollen](wiki:House_Mollen)), [oak](wiki:Oak) leaves ([Oakheart](wiki:House_Oakheart)), beetles ([Bettley](wiki:House_Bettley)), bantams ([Swyft](wiki:House_Swyft)), boars' heads ([Vikary](wiki:House_Vikary)), and tridents ([Condon](wiki:House_Condon) or [Manderly](wiki:House_Manderly)).

## Identity

After Robb Stark breaks his marriage pact, Lord Walder Frey turns to revenge. He conspires with Roose Bolton — Robb's own bannerman, who has wed Walder's granddaughter Walda — and secures the sanction and protection of Lord Tywin Lannister, Hand of the King, to bring House Frey back to the Iron Throne's side. The plot is to lure Robb and his host to the Twins under guest right, for the wedding of Edmure Tully and Roslin Frey, and slaughter them there. Tywin later admits he countenanced the scheme while assigning public blame to Walder. This conspiracy is the covert engine that produces the Red Wedding; it is modeled as a discrete event-hub wired causally INTO the Red Wedding (not as an umbrella parent over it).

> **Note:** the three role edges (Walder Frey COMMANDS_IN, Roose Bolton AGENT_IN, Tywin Lannister COMMANDS_IN) are all ASOS-chapter-grounded. Lothar "Lame Lothar" Frey (the *arranger*) and Ryman Frey were deliberately NOT added — their roles are attested only in the wiki / Merrett Frey's AFFC account, not in an ASOS POV chapter. Add them later if a fuller conspirator roster is wanted (they'd be `AGENT_IN`, evidence_kind wiki / book-pass1 from AFFC).

Still traveling with [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark), [Sandor Clegane](wiki:Sandor_Clegane) gives the [gift of mercy](wiki:Gift_of_mercy) to a dying Piper bowman who had been betrayed by a Bolton soldier. Arya has a dream of her direwolf, [Nymeria](wiki:Nymeria_(direwolf)), recovering a body from a river.(wiki:Red_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos65.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-2) This turns out to be the corpse of [Catelyn Stark](wiki:Catelyn_Stark), and Lord [Beric Dondarrion](wiki:Beric_Dondarrion) gives his life to revive her with the [last kiss](wiki:Last_kiss).(wiki:Red_Wedding.cite_ref-Raffc42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_38-1) Catelyn, now the vengeful Lady Stoneheart, takes leadership of Beric's [brotherhood without banners](wiki:Brotherhood_without_banners), and they kill [Petyr](wiki:Petyr_Frey) and Merrett Frey.(wiki:Red_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasosepilogue.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-7) During the siege of Riverrun, Jaime sees Robb's crown worn by [Ryman's whore](wiki:Queen_of_whores),(wiki:Red_Wedding.cite_ref-Raffc38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-4) and the crown ends up in the grasp of Stoneheart(wiki:Red_Wedding.cite_ref-Raffc42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_38-2) after Ryman's party is ambushed by the outlaws while returning to the Twins.(wiki:Red_Wedding.cite_ref-Raffc44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-3)

Ser [Jared Frey](wiki:Jared_Frey) tells Lord [Wyman Manderly](wiki:Wyman_Manderly), [Lord of White Harbor](wiki:Lord_of_White_Harbor), that the Red Wedding was actually Robb Stark's work. According to the Frey story, Robb changed into a [wolf](wiki:Wolf) as a [warg](wiki:Warg) before the eyes of the Freys and tore out the throat of [Jinglebell](wiki:Aegon_Frey_(son_of_Stevron)), a harmless [simpleton](wiki:Simpleton). Furthermore, he allegedly would have slain Lord Walder if Wyman's son, Ser [Wendel Manderly](wiki:Wendel_Manderly), had not put himself in the way. According to Jared, the other northmen also turned into wolves to join Robb's attack.(wiki:Red_Wedding.cite_ref-Radwd19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_40-1) Wyman supports the Freys and Boltons publicly, but he privately disbelieves their lies.(wiki:Red_Wedding.cite_ref-Radwd29.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_41-0) When Jared and his kin, [Rhaegar](wiki:Rhaegar_Frey) and [Symond](wiki:Symond_Frey), go missing after departing [White Harbor](wiki:White_Harbor), Wyman pleads ignorance about [their fates](wiki:Frey_Pies/Theories).(wiki:Red_Wedding.cite_ref-Radwd46.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-7) Since many lost kin at the Red Wedding, northmen resent the presence of Freys who travel with Lord Bolton to Winterfell for the wedding of his son [Ramsay](wiki:Ramsay_Snow) to "[Arya Stark](wiki:Jeyne_Poole)".(wiki:Red_Wedding.cite_ref-Radwd46.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-8) [Theon Greyjoy](wiki:Theon_Greyjoy), a captive of the Boltons, wishes he had died by Robb's side.(wiki:Red_Wedding.cite_ref-Radwd51.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_42-0)

Thousands of [sparrows](wiki:Sparrow) in [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) are furious about the massacre and its violation of guest right. The [small council](wiki:Small_council) of King [Tommen I Baratheon](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon) discusses how to blame the Freys, and not the Iron Throne, for the Red Wedding,(wiki:Red_Wedding.cite_ref-Raffc17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_43-0) as did Tywin before his death.(wiki:Red_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_23-2) [Qyburn](wiki:Qyburn) suggests once Lord Walder dies, blame should be placed on the Freys that lose the impending fight over succession.(wiki:Red_Wedding.cite_ref-Raffc17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_43-1)

## Quotes

In [133 AC](wiki:133_AC), Winter Fever took Ser [Tyland Lannister](wiki:Tyland_Lannister), who had returned from the [Free Cities](wiki:Free_Cities) the year before, and had been named Hand of the King, a position which he held and fulfilled capably, despite his mutilations. Lord [Roland Westerling](wiki:Roland_Westerling), one of the regents, died in [133 AC](wiki:133_AC) of Winter Fever as well, and was subsequently replaced by Lord [Thaddeus Rowan](wiki:Thaddeus_Rowan).(wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4)
Lord [Unwin Peake](wiki:Unwin_Peake) had felt slighted when he had not been amongst the original seven regents. Yet in [132 AC](wiki:132_AC), he was given Lord Corlys Velaryon's position. He rose to the position of Hand in [133 AC](wiki:133_AC), following the death of Ser Tyland Lannister. In his position of power, he saw his kin hold many high office, and endeavored to weaken his rivals by any means at hand.(wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-5)
King Aegon III had married his cousin, Princess [Jaehaera Targaryen](wiki:Jaehaera_Targaryen), the sole surviving child of King [Aegon II Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_II_Targaryen), following the end of the civil war. She apparently killed herself in [133 AC](wiki:133_AC), when she threw herself out of her window and was impaled on the spikes of [Maegor's Holdfast](wiki:Maegor's_Holdfast). Whilst officially named a suicide, there are some who question the manner of her death. Some whisper that Jaehaera had been murdered, and one of the suspects named was Ser [Mervyn Flowers](wiki:Mervyn_Flowers) of the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard), the [bastard](wiki:Bastard) half-brother of Lord Unwin, who had been the guard at Jaehaera's door when she died. [Court-fool](wiki:Fool) [Mushroom](wiki:Mushroom) suggests that Mervyn did not do the deed himself, but had allowed for someone else to push her out of the window. Maester [Yandel](wiki:Yandel) speculates the assassin was [Tessario the Tiger](wiki:Tessario), a [sellsword](wiki:Sellsword) of the Free Cities in Lord Unwin's service.(wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-6)
Following the death of Queen Jaehaera, Lord Unwin attempted to marry his own daughter to King Aegon. When the regents stopped his attempts, the Hand arranged the [Maiden's Day Ball](wiki:Maiden's_Day_Ball), during which a thousand maids were presented to the king. Ladies [Rhaena](wiki:Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Daemon)) and [Baela Targaryen](wiki:Baela_Targaryen) presented a cousin of theirs, Lady [Daenaera Velaryon](wiki:Daenaera_Velaryon), at the time only six years old. Aegon choose her as his new queen, which greatly frustrated Lord Peake. He attempted to have the king's choice put aside, but the other regents, as well as Aegon, opposed him. Unwin threatened to resign as Hand of the King in an attempt to bend the other regents to his will, but they were only happy to oblige. Thus Lord Peake's resignation was accepted and he was replaced as Hand by Lord [Thaddeus Rowan](wiki:Thaddeus_Rowan) in [134 AC](wiki:134_AC).(wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-7)

Aegon III's younger brother, Prince [Viserys Targaryen](wiki:Viserys_II_Targaryen), was believed dead by many. His return to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) was one of the few joys of Aegon's reign. Having been found in [Lys](wiki:Lys) by Lord [Alyn Velaryon](wiki:Alyn_Velaryon), a huge ransom was paid and the prince was allowed to return to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing). With him, he brought his new wife, [Larra Rogare](wiki:Larra_Rogare), a wealthy daughter of the powerful and ambitious [Rogare family](wiki:Rogare_family). Larra's family owned the [Rogare Bank](wiki:Rogare_Bank), so powerful at the time that it was greater than the [Iron Bank of Braavos](wiki:Iron_Bank_of_Braavos).(wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-9)
The Rogare family soon became involved in the conflicts of this period. Accusations of wrongdoing were spread back and forth by many parties. Lord [Thaddeus Rowan](wiki:Thaddeus_Rowan) was accused of treason and being involved in a plot with the Rogare family and was tortured for information. After these affairs, Ser [Marston Waters](wiki:Marston_Waters) of the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard) became [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), and sent men to arrest Lady Larra after he arrested her brothers, [Lysaro](wiki:Lysaro_Rogare) and [Moredo](wiki:Moredo_Rogare). The young king Aegon defended his brother and his brother's wife and family, however, and refused to give her up, which led to a [secret siege](wiki:Secret_siege) in [Maegor's Holdfast](wiki:Maegor's_Holdfast) for eighteen days. Eventually, recalling his duty, Marston attempted to fulfil his king's command and arrest those who falsely implicated the Rogares and Lord Rowan. Ser Marston himself was killed attempting to arrest his sworn brother, Ser [Mervyn Flowers](wiki:Mervyn_Flowers).(wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-10)

### End of Regency[ ]

Following the betrayal of [Hugh Hammer](wiki:Hugh_Hammer) and [Ulf White](wiki:Ulf_White) during the First Battle of Tumbleton, Queen [Rhaenyra Targaryen](wiki:Rhaenyra_Targaryen) became convinced that none of the [dragonseeds](wiki:Dragonseeds) could be trusted. In [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing), she ordered the arrest of Ser [Addam Velaryon](wiki:Addam_Velaryon), the legitimized heir of Lord [Corlys Velaryon](wiki:Corlys_Velaryon), Rhaenyra's [Hand of the Queen](wiki:Hand_of_the_King). Corlys warned Addam, who fled from the [Dragonpit](wiki:Dragonpit) on his [dragon](wiki:Dragon) [Seasmoke](wiki:Seasmoke). For aiding Addam in his escape, Corlys was arrested, and placed in the [black cells](wiki:Black_cells).(wiki:Riot_of_King's_Landing_(Dance_of_the_Dragons).cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_triumphant.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1) As a result, the Velaryon forces, which made up more than half of Rhaenyra's army, began to abandon her cause. Some joined the [Shepherd](wiki:Shepherd), others returned to [Driftmark](wiki:Driftmark). Those who remained could no longer be trusted. Ser [Denys Woodwright](wiki:Denys_Woodwright) and Ser [Thoron True](wiki:Thoron_True), two of Corlys's sworn swords, attempted to break Corlys out of the dungeons, but were caught and hanged from the walls of the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep).(wiki:Riot_of_King's_Landing_(Dance_of_the_Dragons).cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_overthrown.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)

That same day, Queen [Helaena Targaryen](wiki:Helaena_Targaryen) committed suicide by jumping from her window on top of the spikes that lined the dry moat of [Maegor's Holdfast](wiki:Maegor's_Holdfast). That night, the [smallfolk](wiki:Smallfolk) of King's Landing began to tell each other that Helaena had been murdered, and placed the blame at the feet of Queen Rhaenyra, insisting she had killed Helaena because Prince [Daeron Targaryen](wiki:Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_I)) and the [Hightower](wiki:House_Hightower) army would soon be at the gates. The people whispered that Ser [Luthor Largent](wiki:Luthor_Largent) had flung Helaena from her window on Rhaenyra's order, despite the fact that Largent had been at the barracks of the [City Watch of King's Landing](wiki:City_Watch_of_King's_Landing) at the [Gate of the Gods](wiki:Gate_of_the_Gods) when Helaena died. According to Grand Maester [Munkun ](wiki:Munkun), it was the Shepherd who spread the tale, although [Mushroom](wiki:Mushroom) believes that story originated from Lord [Larys Strong](wiki:Larys_Strong).(wiki:Riot_of_King's_Landing_(Dance_of_the_Dragons).cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_overthrown.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1)

The rumor of the beloved Helaena's murder was quickly believed by the citizens, who had completely turned against the once-beloved Rhaenyra and who were still mindful of the terrible murders of Helaena's sons, [Jaehaerys](wiki:Jaehaerys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aegon_II)) and [Maelor](wiki:Maelor_Targaryen).(wiki:Riot_of_King's_Landing_(Dance_of_the_Dragons).cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_overthrown.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-2)

## Aftermath

## Origins

The riots are caused by lack of [food](wiki:Food) and a sense of dissatisfaction among the general populace during the preparation of [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister), acting [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), for the eventual assault on King's Landing by [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon), a claimant to the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne). With Lord [Mace Tyrell](wiki:Mace_Tyrell) cutting off supplies from the [Reach](wiki:Reach) coming up the [roseroad](wiki:Roseroad), and the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands) awash in blood, King's Landing is supplied mainly by [House Rosby](wiki:House_Rosby) and [House Stokeworth](wiki:House_Stokeworth), whose lands have been largely untouched by the war. This leads to shortages in most basic foods, and most of the incoming provisions are allocated to the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep) and the garrison. A baker is killed in his oven for allegedly charging too much for his bread.(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0)

## Aftermath

The [small council](wiki:Small_council) extends the curfew, bringing more peace to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing).(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Racok44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-1) Food remains scarce, however, and the price of new seafood soars.(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Racok49.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0) Ser [Jacelyn Bywater](wiki:Jacelyn_Bywater) is unsure of the discipline and loyalty of the [gold cloaks](wiki:Gold_cloaks) under his command, and he reveals to [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister) that the [smallfolk](wiki:Smallfolk) mostly blame Tyrion, the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), for the city's troubles instead of young King [Joffrey I Baratheon](wiki:Joffrey_Baratheon).(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Racok41.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-10) Tyrion has a [new High Septon](wiki:High_Septon_(Tyrion's)) named, and Ser [Balon Swann](wiki:Balon_Swann) fills Ser [Preston Greenfield](wiki:Preston_Greenfield)'s place in the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard).(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Racok49.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-1)

[Sansa Stark](wiki:Sansa_Stark) has nightmares of the riot.(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Racok52.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) Queen Regent [Cersei Lannister](wiki:Cersei_Lannister) plans for Prince [Tommen Baratheon](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon) to be discretely moved to [Rosby](wiki:Rosby) for his own safety.(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Racok44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-2) [Tyrek Lannister](wiki:Tyrek_Lannister) remains missing, despite the efforts of Jacelyn, Lord [Varys](wiki:Varys), and Ser [Addam Marbrand](wiki:Addam_Marbrand) to find him.(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Rasos4.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0) Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) notes that Varys was not present to see Princess [Myrcella Baratheon](wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon) off to Dorne, thereby avoiding the riot, and he wonders if Varys had agents abduct Tyrek because the youth had squired for King [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon).(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Raffc27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1)

### Abduction of Lyanna Stark

At the coming of the new year, [Lyanna Stark](wiki:Lyanna_Stark) was abducted by Prince [Rhaegar Targaryen](wiki:Rhaegar_Targaryen) near [Harrenhal](wiki:Harrenhal).(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiaf_the_fall_of_the_dragons:_the_year_of_the_false_spring.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0) Her brother, [Brandon Stark](wiki:Brandon_Stark), was on his way to [Riverrun](wiki:Riverrun) to wed [Catelyn Tully](wiki:Catelyn_Stark), when the news reached him. Brandon rode at once to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) with his companions: [Ethan Glover](wiki:Ethan_Glover), Brandon's squire, [Elbert Arryn](wiki:Elbert_Arryn), nephew to Lord [Jon Arryn](wiki:Jon_Arryn) and the heir of the [Eyrie](wiki:Eyrie), [Kyle Royce](wiki:Kyle_Royce), and [Jeffory Mallister](wiki:Jeffory_Mallister).(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0) Brandon rode for the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep), and outside its gates, unaware that Rhaegar was not present, shouted for him to come out and die. King [Aerys II](wiki:Aerys_II_Targaryen) had them all arrested for plotting the murder of the crown prince, and summoned the fathers of all those who stood accused to court.(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-1) Lord [Rickard Stark](wiki:Rickard_Stark), who had been on his way south with the wedding party for Brandon's wedding,(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rawoiaf_catelyn_tully.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0) went to King's Landing with two hundred men.(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Ragot53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0) None of them returned north. In King's Landing, all of those who stood accused were executed,(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Ragot53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-1) with the sole exception of Ethan Glover.(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Ragot39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-0)

King Aerys did allow for trials, of a sort. Lord Rickard Stark demanded a [trial by battle](wiki:Trial_by_battle), and the king granted his request. Rickard dressed for battle, but Aerys declared fire to be the champion of [House Targaryen](wiki:House_Targaryen). He had Rickard suspended from the rafters in the throne room, where his [pyromancers](wiki:Pyromancers), [Rossart](wiki:Rossart) amongst them, kindled a blaze beneath the Lord of Winterfell. Brandon Stark was brought in as well, and placed in a [Tyroshi](wiki:Tyrosh) strangling device, while a longsword was placed just outside his reach. Brandon was allowed to try and free his father, but the more he struggled to reach the sword, the tighter the cord around his neck became. Brandon strangled himself in an attempt to reach the sword and save his father, while Lord Rickard cooked in his armor.(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-2)

Robert had no wish to marry following the death of his betrothed, [Lyanna Stark](wiki:Lyanna_Stark), but Lord Arryn convinced him otherwise by informing Robert that the realm was in need of an heir. Jon suggested Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister)'s daughter, [Cersei](wiki:Cersei_Lannister), as the marriage would ensure Tywin's support, should [Viserys Targaryen](wiki:Viserys_Targaryen) ever attempt to win his throne back.(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Ragot30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_43-1) Robert and Cersei married in [284 AC](wiki:284_AC).(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-56) Maester [Yandel](wiki:Yandel) described the marriage as Robert's "first act" as king.(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiaf_the_glorious_reign.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_57-0)

### Rumored Dornish Resistance

The deaths of Princess [Elia Martell](wiki:Elia_Martell) and her two young children, [Rhaenys](wiki:Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Rhaegar)) and [Aegon Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Rhaegar)), infuriated [House Martell](wiki:House_Martell). Prince [Oberyn Martell](wiki:Oberyn_Martell), Elia's younger brother, attempted to raise [Dorne](wiki:Dorne) for the exiled Viserys. Messages were sent by both raven and messenger, though their contents have remained a secret.(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rasos53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_58-0) [Jon Arryn](wiki:Jon_Arryn), the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), went to Dorne the year after the rebellion,(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rasos38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_59-0) returned the bones of Prince [Lewyn Martell](wiki:Lewyn_Martell), and sat down with [Doran Martell](wiki:Doran_Martell), the [Prince of Dorne](wiki:Prince_of_Dorne) and the older brother of Elia and Oberyn. During his stay in [Sunspear](wiki:Sunspear), Jon was "questioned closely" regarding the fates of Elia and her children,(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rasos38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_59-1) and a hundred others were questioned as well.(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rasos38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_59-2) While all talk of war ended in Dorne after Jon's visit,(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rasos53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_58-1) Doran continued to plot a Targaryen restoration in secret,(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Raffc41.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_60-0) working closely together with Oberyn.(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Radwd38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_61-0)

## Aftermath

After hearing of unrest in King's Landing against the rule of Queen [Rhaenyra Targaryen](wiki:Rhaenyra_Targaryen), younger nobles such as Ser [Jon Roxton](wiki:Jon_Roxton), Ser [Roger Corne](wiki:Roger_Corne), and Lord Peake called for advancing on the capital, but Hobert preached caution and the Two Betrayers remained stubborn. When Hugh crowned himself king and had his men attack Roger, thirteen loyalist lords known as the [Caltrops](wiki:Caltrops) plotted against the two baseborn dragonriders.(wiki:Second_Battle_of_Tumbleton.cite_ref-Rtpatq.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)

Meanwhile, the baseborn dragonrider Ser [Addam Velaryon](wiki:Addam_Velaryon), whom Rhaenyra had condemned after hearing of the Two Betrayers, was resolved to defend his honor by retaking Tumbleton from Ulf and Hugh. He fled from King's Landing atop [Seasmoke](wiki:Seasmoke) and allegedly sought counsel from the [Green Men](wiki:Green_Men) on the [Isle of Faces](wiki:Isle_of_Faces). Addam set out to recruit a new army from the rivermen, by visiting every castle great or small and scraping together survivors of previous battles, along with old men and young boys. A brave and charming young man and glib of tongue, Addam succeeded in convincing many lords to contribute what aid they could - due to a combination of being impressed by his dragon, and being terrified of what green victory in the war would mean for them after the horrific sack of Tumbleton. Among them were [Benjicot Blackwood](wiki:Benjicot_Blackwood), the 12 year old Lord of Raventree Hall (whose father had died in the [Battle of the Burning Mill](wiki:Battle_of_the_Burning_Mill), and who later fought in the Fishfeed); [Sabitha Vypren](wiki:Sabitha_Vypren), widowed Lady of the Twins whose husband Lord Frey had died fighting in the [Battle by the Lakeshore](wiki:Battle_by_the_Lakeshore), who was now also joined by her father and brothers from [House Vypren](wiki:House_Vypren); Lord [Stanton Piper](wiki:Stanton_Piper) and Lord [Joseth Smallwood](wiki:Joseth_Smallwood) (who had survived defeat in the earlier [Battle at Acorn Hall](wiki:Battle_at_Acorn_Hall)); Lord [Derrick Darry](wiki:Derrick_Darry) (leading the survivors of Castle Darry after [Vhagar](wiki:Vhagar) burned it, killing the original Lord Darry); and Lord [Lyonel Deddings](wiki:Lyonel_Deddings). [Hugo Vance](wiki:Hugo_Vance_(lord)), the new Lord of [House Vance of Wayfarer's Rest](wiki:House_Vance_of_Wayfarer's_Rest) after the death of [Tristan Vance](wiki:Tristan_Vance) at the [Battle of the Red Fork](wiki:Battle_of_the_Red_Fork), came with three hundred of his own men plus [Black Trombo](wiki:Black_Trombo)'s company of [Myrish](wiki:Myr) sellswords (Hugo and Trombo had earlier fought in the [Butcher's Ball](wiki:Butcher's_Ball), destroying Ser [Criston Cole](wiki:Criston_Cole)'s trapped army). Most notably, [House Tully](wiki:House_Tully) finally joined the war, with its army full strength and fresh. Seasmoke's arrival and the fate of Tumbleton finally convinced the reluctant [Elmo Tully](wiki:Elmo_Tully) to end their neutrality, against the wishes of his bedridden grandsire Lord [Grover Tully](wiki:Grover_Tully). Altogether, Addam collected together a new army of rivermen nearly 4,000 strong.(wiki:Second_Battle_of_Tumbleton.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_overthrown.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0)

## Narrative Arc

When Lord [Alyn Cockshaw](wiki:Alyn_Cockshaw), Daemon's friend, attempted to kill Duncan out of jealousy, Duncan kicked Alyn into a well to drown, an event observed by Ser [Maynard Plumm](wiki:Maynard_Plumm). Meanwhile, Duncan's squire Egg, actually Prince [Aegon Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_V_Targaryen), revealed his identity to Maester [Lothar](wiki:Lothar), who then informed Lords Butterwell and Frey. The Frey retinue immediately departed Whitewalls to return to the Twins, and Ambrose admitted his doubts about the conspiracy to Duncan and insisted that Lord Peake had plotted everything. Duncan then slew Ser Tommard in single combat when he tried to take Prince Aegon into captivity.(wiki:Second_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-5)

Within the great hall of Whitewalls, Duncan publicly accused John the Fiddler of being Daemon and alleged that the egg that had been found in Glendon's saddlebags was actually a painted stone planted by order of Lord Peake; the real dragon egg was still missing. Daemon had been unaware of the plots, and was unsure who to believe. He ordered a trial by joust, declaring that he would personally face Glendon.(wiki:Second_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-6)

Many wedding guests disappeared before the joust the next morning, including Ser Maynard. Daemon washed the black dye from his hair to reveal silver and gold [Valyrian](wiki:Valyrians) hair, and his horse bore the sigil of [House Blackfyre](wiki:House_Blackfyre). Glendon, who had been tortured in the dungeon, unhorsed Daemon on the first pass.(wiki:Second_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-7)

## Origins

During the [Lysene Spring](wiki:Lysene_Spring), Prince [Viserys Targaryen](wiki:Viserys_II_Targaryen)'s wife, Lady [Larra of Lys](wiki:Larra_Rogare), and her family from [House Rogare](wiki:House_Rogare) were accused of being part of a plot to overthrow King Aegon. Lord [Thaddeus Rowan](wiki:Thaddeus_Rowan), the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), was also implicated in the conspiracy and was arrested and taken to the dungeons where the [Lord Confessor](wiki:Lord_Confessor), Lord [George Graceford](wiki:George_Graceford) began torturing him. Ser [Marston Waters](wiki:Marston_Waters), the [Lord Commander of the Kingsguard](wiki:Lord_Commander_of_the_Kingsguard), was appointed the new Hand by the council of [regents](wiki:Regent) and he ordered the arrest of Larra and her kin.(wiki:Secret_siege.cite_ref-Rfabthe_lysene_spring_and_the_end_of_regency.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)

Ser [Amaury Peake](wiki:Amaury_Peake) of the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard) along with a dozen [men-at-arms](wiki:Men-at-arms) went to [Maegor's Holdfast](wiki:Maegor's_Holdfast) to arrest her, but Prince Viserys and King [ Aegon](wiki:Aegon_III_Targaryen) refused to give her up. Viserys planted an axe upon the drawbridge and warned Peake and his men not to cross it, before retreating back into the castle. When Amaury's men passed the axe, [Sandoq the Shadow](wiki:Sandoq) emerged and slew all the guards and Ser Amaury himself. After Sandoq was done, he retreated within the holdfast and the king commanded the portcullis be lowered and the drawbridge raised. King Aegon, Prince Viserys, Queen [Daenaera](wiki:Daenaera_Velaryon), Lady Larra Rogare, [Mushroom](wiki:Mushroom), several ladies in waiting, a few guardsmen and Sandoq would remain within the castle within a castle for eighteen days. Ser [Raynard Ruskyn](wiki:Raynard_Ruskyn) of the Kingsguard was within the holdfast too, but had been overwhelmed by Larra's [Lysene](wiki:Lys) guards and made a prisoner.(wiki:Secret_siege.cite_ref-Rfabthe_lysene_spring_and_the_end_of_regency.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)

The armies of the Yunkai'i descend on Astapor.(wiki:Siege_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Radwd16.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-1) When Cleon commands the Astapori to march out against the Yunkai'i, the Butcher King is slain by his own soldiers. Another man declares himself [Cleon the Second](wiki:Cleon_the_Second), but his throat is cut eight days later. That man's killer claims the crown, as does the first Cleon's concubine. ## Identity

Immediately upon Joffrey's death at the wedding feast, Cersei Lannister accuses her brother Tyrion of poisoning the king, and Tyrion is seized and imprisoned. This is an **accusation and arrest**, not a factual poisoning by Tyrion — he is framed (the true plot is Littlefinger's, with the Tyrells). The graph models Tyrion here only as the accused/seized party. The accusation is the engine that drives Tyrion to trial.
